reasoning: Query is specifically about fireworks (花火大会) but get_festivals with no filter returns 312 generic intangible cultural festivals — none of which appear to be fireworks events. The results show shrine festivals, dance traditions, etc. The tool call ignores the specific 花火 constraint entirely.
missing_data: Actual fireworks festival entries (Sumida, Nagaoka, Tsuchiura, etc.) — none surfaced. Date/season data also absent.
improvement_hint: Add keyword filtering to get_festivals so querying 'hanabi' or '花火' surfaces fireworks-specific events. Alternatively, tag festival items with event_type so agents can filter post-retrieval.

reasoning: Same issue as L3-01 in Japanese: query asks for 花火 (fireworks) but the tool returns 312 generic cultural festivals with no fireworks. Lang=ja correctly applied, but the topic mismatch is the same fatal flaw.
missing_data: Fireworks-specific festival entries with dates and locations.
improvement_hint: Support keyword search parameter in get_festivals, or introduce a festival_type tag ('fireworks', 'parade', 'dance') enabling accurate filtering.

reasoning: Query asks about snow festivals in Hokkaido but the tool returns 0 Hokkaido festivals (count=0). It falls back to national_heritage which is irrelevant (Dainichido Bugaku in Akita, Kawagoe Hikawa in Saitama, etc.). None relate to snow festivals. Complete intent failure compounded by irrelevant fallback results.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival (さっぽろ雪まつり), Asahikawa Winter Festival — key Hokkaido snow events absent from corpus.
improvement_hint: Index major recurring festivals (Sapporo Snow Festival) as first-class items. When prefecture filter yields 0, do not fall back to national_heritage without relevance filtering.

reasoning: Query is about experiencing rural village life (田舎暮らし体験) in Chinese, but get_japan_heritage returns broad cultural heritage stories (Asuka period women, Kamakura history). The content is historically rich but none specifically maps to rural countryside living experiences. Tool choice is wrong for this intent.
missing_data: Rural homestay experiences, satoyama areas, agricultural villages like Shirakawa-go or Iya Valley.
improvement_hint: search_area with q='田舎' or q='農家民宿' would be more appropriate. get_japan_heritage needs theme filtering for 'rural life' or 'satoyama'.

reasoning: Query asks for autumn foliage spots in Korean. search_area('紅葉') returns 83 matches with good variety: Momijidani Park (Hiroshima), Sakurajima, park/garden/valley types. Prominence scores present. Heritage designations add value. Lang=ko applied. Main gap: results skew toward places named 紅葉 rather than famous foliage viewing spots (Kyoto, Nikko not surfaced).
missing_data: Famous koyo spots like Nikko, Kyoto temples — not surfaced because query is lexical match, not semantic.
improvement_hint: Add semantic/BM25 hybrid to retrieve famous foliage destinations not literally named '紅葉'.

reasoning: Query seeks quiet meditation temples, not touristy. search_area('修行') retrieves 79 results but they are mixed: actual temples (Shugyoji Tokyo, Manjushuin Kyoto) buried under noisy municipal scrape entries (STAY YAMAGATA tourism portal in Chinese/Traditional Chinese, cookie-consent-only pages). Top results are not actionable for the meditation intent.
missing_data: Zazen retreat info, shukubo (temple lodging), specific meditation programs. Coordination of accessibility.
wrong_data: Description of Q3076940 (Manjushuin) says 'anglican priest' — wrong data in description_en.
improvement_hint: Rank wikidata attraction results above low-quality municipal scrape cookie-notice pages. Filter or demote scrape results with trivially short descriptions.

reasoning: Query asks where to see northern lights in Japan (aurora borealis) — a niche topic. Results are mostly noise: Oita hot spring business site, DMO plan chunks about Furano/Yoshino tourism strategy, Okinawa mountain, Sanrio Puroland. No actual aurora-viewing content. Aurora is theoretically visible in Hokkaido in rare conditions but the corpus has nothing relevant.
missing_data: Any information about aurora visibility in Hokkaido (Abashiri, Rishiri etc.) — completely absent.
improvement_hint: Add coverage of rare natural phenomena (aurora, bioluminescence). Could include a note that aurora is not reliably visible in Japan to set expectations for agents.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for 100-year-old historic houses to sleep in (古民家), but get_hotels(Gifu) returns generic hotel listings: Airbnb apartment, hostel, standard hotels, Route Inn. None are identified as machiya/kominka heritage properties. Gifu (Takayama/Shirakawa-go area) does have kominka lodging but they are not surfaced. Tool choice OK but data lacks lodging-type granularity.
missing_data: Kominka-specific lodging data, traditional gassho-zukuri farmhouse stays in Shirakawa-go area.
improvement_hint: Add 'kominka', 'machiya', 'traditional_farmhouse' lodging subtypes to hotel schema. Allow get_hotels to filter by lodging character.

reasoning: Thai query asks for off-the-beaten-path spots away from tourists. search_area('桜') returns 165 results in Thai but the top hits are named '桜' literally: Sakurada-mon (Imperial Palace gate), Sakuragi Station, Sakurai shrine — not cherry blossom viewing spots per se. The 'hidden gems' constraint is not handled (no popularity/crowding filter). Sakurajima (prominence 0.9) visible in results.
missing_data: Lesser-known cherry blossom spots, crowd-level data or rural location indicators.
improvement_hint: Expose a 'max_prominence' filter so agents can request low-prominence results for off-the-beaten-path queries.

reasoning: Photography/dramatic landscapes query returns 52 matches for '絶景' but top results are dominated by low-quality municipal scrape items: a photo download page, a cookie-consent-only Iwate feature page. The Niigata page has good content but is buried after noise. Ehime content is good but seasonal/specific. High-drama landscape icons (Fuji, Tottori dunes, Yakushima) not retrieved.
missing_data: Iconic dramatic landscape sites (Mt. Fuji, Tottori Sand Dunes, Yakushima, Shiretoko).
improvement_hint: Deprioritize scrape results that are navigation/portal pages without substantive spot descriptions. Improve prominence-based ranking.

reasoning: Arabic query for outdoor hot springs (露天風呂) in nature, not hotel-based. Returns 72 results including Iwate onsen page with authentic wild-style onsen descriptions (Sukawa onsen 6000L/min, Haraigawa onsen family baths in mountain setting). Addresses outdoor natural bathing reasonably. Lang=ar applied. Main constraint gap: 'not in hotels' distinction is not enforced but some results are nature-oriented.
missing_data: Wild/free outdoor onsen spots (hitou), season/access information.
improvement_hint: Tag onsen spots as 'indoor_facility', 'outdoor_natural', 'free_wild' to allow constraint matching on the 'not hotel' preference.

reasoning: Wild dolphin swimming query returns 60 results but the top hit is Kyoto Tourism Ambassador page (irrelevant). Second result is Noto-jima Aquarium (captive dolphins, not wild swimming). Actual wild dolphin swim locations (Mikura-jima, Amami Oshima) not retrieved. Heavy noise from unrelated municipal scrape results.
missing_data: Wild dolphin interaction spots: Mikurajima (Tokyo), Amami Oshima, Nagahama (Ehime). Access and season details.
improvement_hint: Improve municipal scrape quality filtering. Add wildlife-experience tags to attractions so wild vs captive dolphin content can be distinguished.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional unmodernized architecture (重伝建 intent) but get_japan_heritage returns 104 unfiltered Japan Heritage stories starting with Asuka Women and Kamakura — which are about history/culture broadly, not specifically architectural streetscapes. The tool could work but without q-filtering or theme matching, the agent gets no prioritization toward architecture topics.
missing_data: Traditional architecture districts like Shirakawa-go, Tsumago, Chiran, Uchiko — not surfaced in visible results.
improvement_hint: Pass q='伝統的建造物' or theme='建築' to get_japan_heritage to surface architecture-focused stories. Relevance scoring should kick in for keyword-matched queries.

reasoning: Vietnamese query for Ainu culture experience in Hokkaido. search_area('アイヌ') returns 94 excellent, directly relevant results: National Ainu Museum (Upopoy, Shiraoi), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Hokkaido Ainu Center, Kawamura Kaneto Museum, Kusharo Kotan — all real, well-documented, Hokkaido-located Ainu facilities. Cultural landscape designation included. Very strong coverage.
missing_data: Opening hours, admission fees, cultural program scheduling.
wrong_data: heritage_designations_labels for first result shows 'Q64576748' (unresolved QID label) instead of readable label.
improvement_hint: Resolve all heritage QID labels to human-readable strings to avoid agent confusion.

reasoning: Buddhist cliff statues (磨崖仏) query returns 75 results with excellent top hits: Usuki Stone Buddhas (National Treasure, Special Historic Site), Oya-dera Magaibutsu (Tochigi), Kiyomizu Magaibutsu (Kagoshima). All are real, well-heritage-labeled, geographically spread. Prominence scores and multiple heritage designations present. Outstanding retrieval for this niche topic.
missing_data: Access difficulty/trail conditions, visiting hours.
wrong_data: 達磨寺 (Daruma Temple, Gunma) appears in results — it's a temple not a cliff statue, partial noise.
improvement_hint: Improve precision by distinguishing '磨崖仏' (cliff-carved statues) from temples with '磨崖' in name via better entity typing.

reasoning: Query asks for volcano hikes from Tokyo in a day. search_area('火山') returns results but first result is a Fukuoka mountain called '火山' (Hiyama, unrelated), then Aso Caldera content in Chinese from Kumamoto (not day-trip from Tokyo). The Tokyo constraint is not handled; Aso is ~900km away and not a day trip from Tokyo. Mt. Fuji (volcano, day-trip feasible) not surfaced.
missing_data: Fuji-san, Izu Oshima, Hakone Owakudani — all volcanic, accessible from Tokyo in a day.
wrong_data: Presenting Kumamoto Aso content for a Tokyo day-trip query is a travel feasibility failure.
improvement_hint: Add proximity/region parameter handling. When 'from Tokyo' is in query, filter or prioritize Kanto/nearby results by prefecture_code proximity.

reasoning: Indonesian query about eating freshly made tofu at a temple. search_area('豆腐') returns mostly noise: Fukui global website, Chinese ryokan, Kumamoto food/tourism pages, MAFF GI product (Iwadeyama frozen tofu). No temple-tofu experience content found. The niche intent (寺 + 豆腐) requires combining Buddhist temple with tofu cuisine — e.g. Kyoto shojin ryori — which is not captured.
missing_data: Shojin ryori (Buddhist cuisine) experiences, temple tofu restaurants in Kyoto, Arashiyama tofu culture.
improvement_hint: Index shojin ryori as a cuisine category. Add experience-type tags to allow queries combining 'temple' + 'tofu' or 'vegetarian Buddhist cuisine'.

reasoning: Query for fishing villages frozen in time. search_area('漁港') returns 81 results with some useful content: Teradomari fish market (Niigata), Kesennuma port town description with rias coastline details, Kitakyushu sea features. Lang=en applied. However the 'frozen in time / historic' constraint is not addressed — results mix modern fishing ports and historic ones without differentiation.
missing_data: Historic fishing villages like Ine no Funaya (Kyoto), Gunkanjima (Nagasaki), traditional port districts.
improvement_hint: Add heritage/traditional tags to fishing port attractions.荒船風穴 appearing in results (cave spring) is irrelevant noise — improve result filtering.

reasoning: Query about learning Japanese papermaking from a master. get_local_specialty(craft) returns 231 items with METI-designated traditional crafts including textiles. While washi (和紙) is not visible in the truncated portion, the tool is correctly chosen and the dataset is rich and authoritative. Nibutani Attus, Oitama Tsumugi etc. are real, well-described crafts with production area info. The specific 'washi' item likely appears later in the 231-item response.
missing_data: Washi-specific entries not visible in truncated view; experience/workshop booking info absent.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword filter q='和紙' to get_local_specialty so washi results surface at top rather than requiring agent to scan 231 items.

reasoning: Query for period-drama scenery (時代劇) mapped to '宿場' (post towns) — smart lexical choice. Returns 89 results with excellent content: Nagano Kisoji post towns (Subarasuku, Nojirijuku), Yanagimachi Ueda post-town description (300m-long Edo street), Shizuoka Tokaido cycling route. Lang=ja. Heritage designations on Akasaka Detached Palace. Good geographic diversity.
missing_data: Tsumago, Magome (most famous post towns) not visible in truncated output but likely present further in results.
improvement_hint: Re-rank Tsumago/Magome (prominence=high) to top positions for '宿場' queries given their fame as preserved post-towns.

reasoning: Query for uncrowded beaches. search_area('ビーチ') returns 79 results with named beaches and coordinates: Naminoue Beach (Okinawa), Hamasaka Beach (Hyogo), Sunayama Beach, Yonehara Beach (Ishigaki). Good geographic variety. However the 'not crowded' constraint cannot be handled — all results include famous beaches and no crowding/popularity data available. Theme parks (Minami Chita Beach Land, Oiso Long Beach) dominate top slots.
missing_data: Crowd/season data, remote island beaches, beaches with low prominence scores prioritized.
improvement_hint: Theme park 'beach' attractions should be tagged differently from natural beaches. Allow max_prominence filtering for off-beaten-path requests.

reasoning: Query for local izakaya drinking in old alleyways. search_area('横丁') returns 79 but top results are a small park in Shinagawa, a Saga tourism page in Chinese, an amusement park in Yamaguchi, a dessert alley in Ikebukuro — not izakaya-focused. Good spots like Ameyoko, Omoide Yokocho not surfaced. Results mostly irrelevant to the drinking-with-locals intent.
missing_data: Omoide Yokocho (Tokyo), Hoppy Street, regional izakaya alleys like Fukuoka Nakasu.
improvement_hint: Add 'izakaya', 'drinking_alley', 'shotengai' type tags. Improve ranking so famous 横丁 drinking districts surface above parks and amusement attractions named 横丁.

reasoning: German query for rural railway travel. search_area('ローカル線') returns 57 results with relevant content: Nagano Ueda Bessho Line art trip (detailed description with schedule), Ibaraki nemophila route with Kashima Coastal Railway, Aomori Tsugaru DMO plan mentioning two local railways. Lang=de applied. Good coverage of actual local lines with travel context.
missing_data: Line names with operational status, route maps, ticket pricing. Amami access info is interesting but off-topic.
improvement_hint: Index specific ローカル線 entries as named railway products with route, operating company, and scenic highlights as structured fields.

reasoning: Firefly viewing query returns 73 results. Top relevant content: Hotaru-no-sato park (Hyogo), Toyota Firefly Museum in Shimonoseki (with river cruise detail — June 5-21 schedule, pricing ¥2000 adult), Kanazawa Kenroku-en firefly viewing event (dates listed for 2026). Excellent specificity with real event dates and prices. Good geographic spread. Lang=en applied.
missing_data: Genji vs Heike firefly species information, optimal viewing months by region.
improvement_hint: Surface the 'Hotaru-no-sato' park name in English to improve agent usability. Consider tagging spots with optimal season metadata.

reasoning: Russian query for where to see cranes wintering in Japan. search_area('鶴') returns 165 results but top results are Tsurugaoka Hachimangu shrine (Kamakura), Kakurinji temple buildings, Tsuruma Park (Nagoya) — all named '鶴' for unrelated historical/cultural reasons. Actual crane wintering spots (Izumi City, Kagoshima — world's largest crane congregation) and Kushiro wetlands not surfaced.
missing_data: Izumi City Kagoshima (10,000+ cranes winter), Kushiro Shitsugen for red-crowned crane, Arasaki crane sanctuary.
improvement_hint: Add wildlife/nature tags to attractions so migratory birds and nature observation can be queried semantically. Tag Izumi City and Arasaki as crane wintering sites explicitly.
